headline: Review Preview: What, Me Worry?
summary: It a pattern that’s become all too familiar, stocks rose to start the week, but the question is whether they can stay there as the war in Iran remains a flashpoint.  The Dow Jones Industrial Average and the S&P 500 both added 0.4% Monday, while the Nasdaq Composite rose 0.5%.  “The real issue is that even if there’s a climbdown, or TACO, the risk environment has already shifted in a way that leaves markets exposed to sharp, disorderly moves,” warns deVere Group Chief Executive Nigel Green.

headline: Can Delta (DAL) Turn Its Refinery Hedge Into a Lasting Advantage Amid Fuel Volatility?
summary: Delta Air Lines recently faced soaring jet fuel costs and potential shortages linked to the Iran war, even as it prepared to report its first-quarter 2026 earnings and highlighted its refinery ownership as a partial buffer against fuel price spikes. Amid industry-wide margin pressure, Delta has attracted attention for its focus on premium and corporate travel, diversified revenue streams and comments from management and analysts suggesting it may be less exposed to fuel volatility than many...
